diagnosis: The playbook command passed --count 1000, but the actual Scapy send rate is ~27 pkt/s (not the configured 100 pkt/s), so 1000 packets takes ~37s — exceeding the 30s timeout. Fixed by reducing the count in docs/PLAYBOOK.md (the parsed scenario command) from 1000 to 500, and aligning the argparse default in src/attacks/icmp_flood.py to 500. At ~27 pkt/s, 500 packets completes in ~18s, well within 30s.

files_changed.description: Reduced --count from 1000 to 500 in the icmp-flood bash example; this is the source the playbook runner parses to build the scenario command
Reduced argparse default for --count from 1000 to 500 to be consistent with the registry and playbook command

notes: The registry (matcha/registry.py line 190) already had count default=500 from a prior fix attempt. The remaining issue was that the PLAYBOOK.md command explicitly passed --count 1000, overriding the registry default. No Docker rebuild is needed; the fix takes effect on the next scenario run.
